zh.lifeSummary: 當Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon於大約1079年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多時，她的父親Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia40歲，母親Constance de Bourgogne34歲。她和Fernando Fernández de Carrión至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1135年2月20日享年57歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安薩哈奧。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon于大约1079年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多时，她的父亲Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia40岁，母亲Constance de Bourgogne34岁。她和Fernando Fernández de Carrión至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1135年2月20日享年57岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安薩哈奧。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castille
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcee, Antoine, Armand, Maudry, Michel, Monique, Normand, Remy, Renella.
Americanized form of Catalan   Castell  , under the French influence in LA.
French: habitational name from the French name for Castile in Spain (see   Castilla  ).

variants.name: Castell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Roberto, Amarilys, Carlos, Eduardo, Estevan, Francisco, Guadalupe, Humberto, Jorge, Manuel, Mateo, Pablo.
English and Scottish: variant of   Castle  .

Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Asgaill ‘son of Ásketill’, an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. The Manx surname is cognate with Scottish Gaelic McAskill .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Leon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Elvira
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name of Germanic (Visigothic) origin , very common in the Middle Ages. It was not used in the English-speaking world until the 19th century, when it was made familiar as the name of the long-suffering wife of  Don Juan , both in  Mozart's  opera Don Giovanni (  1789 ) and in  Byron's  satirical epic poem Don Juan (  1819–24 ). In his comedy Blithe Spirit  Noel Coward  used it as the name of  Charles Condomine's  first wife, who returns to haunt him. It is also the name of the heroine of the Swedish film Elvira Madigan (  1967 ), directed by  Bo Widerberg , a romantic tragedy about a pair of lovers who would rather die than be separated.

nameMeanings.name: Alfonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: probably a compound of adal ‘noble’ + funs ‘ready’; a traditional royal name.

variants.name: Alonso
meanings: Variant of   Alfonso .

en.lifeSummary: When Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon was born about 1079, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, her father, Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia, was 40 and her mother, Constance de Bourgogne, was 34. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Fernando Fernández de Carrión. She died on 20 February 1135, at the age of 57, and was buried in Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon est née environ 1079, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Constance de Bourgogne, avait 34 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Fernando Fernández de Carrión. Elle est décédée le 20 février 1135, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterrée à Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon um 1079 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia 40 und ihre Mutter Constance de Bourgogne 34. Sie hatte mit Fernando Fernández de Carrión mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. Februar 1135 im Alter von 57 Jahren und wurde in Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon è nata il circa 1079 nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, sua padre, Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Constance de Bourgogne, aveva 34 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Fernando Fernández de Carrión. Morì il 20 febbraio 1135, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolta a Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ko.lifeSummary: Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon(이)가 대략 1079년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constance de Bourgogne(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 Fernando Fernández de Carrión(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1135년 2월 20일 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 레o 사하그u에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon nasceu aproximadamente 1079, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Constance de Bourgogne, tinha 34 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Fernando Fernández de Carrión. Ela faleceu em 20 de fevereiro de 1135, com 57 anos, e foi sepultada em Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon родилась около 1079 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, её отцу по имени Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance de Bourgogne, 34. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Fernando Fernández de Carrión. Она умерла 20 февраля 1135 г. в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности сахагuн, леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Countess Elvira Alfonso de Castille and Leon nació aproximadamente 1079, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, su papá, Rey Alfonso VI de Leon, Castilla y Galicia, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Constance de Bourgogne, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Fernando Fernández de Carrión. Murió el 20 de febrero de 1135, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultada en Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, España.
